reasoning: Sanchez cosponsored the Strengthening Medicare for Patients and Providers Act (H.R. 2474, 2023) and voted for ACA expansions and the Inflation Reduction Act (2022). She supports expanding Medicare benefits to include dental, vision, and hearing and enabling drug price negotiation, reflecting a public option/expansion approach rather than full single-payer.

Sanchez voted for the Women's Health Protection Act codifying abortion access at all stages and has consistently voted against restrictions on federal funding for abortions. She received a 0% rating from the National Right to Life Committee and opposes all abortion restrictions.

As Ways and
Means Trade Subcommittee Ranking Member, Sanchez introduced the Stopping a Rogue President on Trade Act (H.R. 2888, April 2025) to rescind most Trump tariffs and require congressional approval for new ones. She supports targeted trade enforcement through USMCA mechanisms but opposes broad protectionist tariffs.

Sanchez introduced the Responsible Estate Tax Act and supports eliminating the payroll tax cap for Social Security. During the 2025 Ways and Means markup she stated 'Don't buy the bullshit that tax cuts for the rich create more jobs' and has repeatedly denounced the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act as a 'tax scam' benefiting billionaires.

Sanchez is co-founder and Vice Chair of the Congressional LGBT Equality Caucus. She voted for the Respect for Marriage Act (December 2022) repealing DOMA and introduced the Social Security Equality Act to extend benefits to same-sex couples. She has consistently supported full marriage equality with federal benefits since 2008.

Sanchez co-introduced the Equality Act (2017, 2019, 2021) which explicitly limits the Religious Freedom Restoration Act as a defense against anti-discrimination claims. She prioritizes anti-discrimination protections over religious exemptions while the Respect for Marriage Act she supported included some negotiated religious liberty safeguards.

Sanchez was named a 'Trans Equality Champion' by Advocates for Trans Equality for the 118th Congress and voted against H.R. 734 (Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act) which would ban transgender girls from school sports. She cosponsored the Equality Act extending civil rights protections based on gender identity.

Sanchez voted for the Ukraine Security Supplemental Appropriations Act (H.R. 5692, April 2024) and supported Ukraine aid in the FY2023 omnibus. In December 2022 she stated the U.S. 'must continue to stand with' Ukraine and provide 'critical aid' to 'ensure a democratic future for Ukraine.'

Sanchez opposes Medicare privatization and supports expanding Medicare to include dental, vision, and hearing services. She voted for the Inflation Reduction Act enabling Medicare drug price negotiation and cosponsored H.R. 676 (Medicare for All) in 2017 but has focused recent efforts on expanding existing Medicare benefits and lowering eligibility age.

Sanchez voted for the Inflation Reduction Act (2022) calling it 'the largest-ever federal investment in fighting climate change' and co-introduced the Renewable Natural Gas Incentive Act (2025). Her official website states she fights to 'reduce and eliminate greenhouse gas emissions' through clean energy investment rather than an immediate drilling ban.

Sanchez is lead author of the Voting Rights Advancement Act restoring pre-clearance requirements and voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) in 2019 and 2021 mandating automatic voter registration, expanded early voting, and mail-in voting. She also voted for the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act.

Sanchez introduced the U.S. Citizenship Act of 2023 creating an 'earned roadmap to citizenship for all 11 million undocumented immigrants.' She voted against the Laken Riley Act (H.R. 29, 2025) and consistently supports the DREAM Act. She supports 'smart border security' alongside legal pathways rather than mass deportation.

Sanchez introduced the Strengthening Social Security Act (H.R. 3261, May 2023) to increase benefits across-the-board, phase out the taxable income cap, and switch to CPI-E for COLAs. She serves as Ranking Member of the Ways and Means Social Security Subcommittee and opposes any privatization.

Sanchez voted for the Inflation Reduction Act (2022) praising its goal to 'reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 40 percent by 2030.' She supports clean energy tax credits and introduced bipartisan legislation for energy efficiency and renewable natural gas. Her approach emphasizes gradual clean energy investment rather than declaring a climate emergency.

Sanchez received a 100% rating from the NAACP, is lead author of the
Voting Rights Advancement Act, co-founded the
Congressional LGBT Equality Caucus, and introduced the Equality Act to expand civil rights protections. She consistently votes to strengthen enforcement of civil rights laws and address systemic discrimination.

Sanchez introduced legislation to improve veteran access to affordable housing tax credits (H.R. 9788, 2024) and the Fair Housing for Disabled Veterans Act (H.R. 5529, 2025). She voted for the Build Back Better Act and American Rescue Plan including housing investments and supports robust funding for McKinney-Vento homeless assistance.

Sanchez voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) in 2019 and 2021 which includes the DISCLOSE Act for dark money transparency, a small-dollar matching system for public financing, and expanded prohibition on foreign campaign spending. She supports full disclosure of campaign finances and lobbyist disclosures.

Sanchez introduced the U.S. Citizenship Act of 2023 which creates pathways to citizenship for 11 million undocumented immigrants, doubles family-preference visas, raises per-country caps, and eliminates LGBTQ+ discrimination in immigration. She supports the DREAM Act and TPS protections while advocating for dramatically expanded legal immigration.

Sanchez voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) in 2019 and 2021 which requires every state to establish independent nonpartisan redistricting commissions with no elected officials, bans partisan gerrymandering, and mandates transparency with equal numbers of Democrats, Republicans, and unaffiliated members.

Sherman supported passage of the ACA and voted for the Inflation Reduction Act (2022) which capped insulin costs and enabled Medicare drug price negotiation. His focus is expanding coverage through public options alongside private insurance.

Sherman opposed NAFTA, CAFTA, and MFN for China, arguing they cost American jobs. Sherman cosponsored and voted for the Respect for Marriage Act (signed December 13, 2022) codifying same-sex marriage protections. He is a Congressional LGBTQ+ Equality Caucus member with a 100% HRC rating and stated: 'I believe that government should allow all consenting adults, regardless of sexual orientation, to enjoy the benefits of legally-recognized marriage.'

Sherman supports the Do No Harm Act clarifying that RFRA cannot be used to discriminate against protected classes. His official website states it is 'more important than ever that Congress pass the Do No Harm Act' to prevent religious beliefs from being used to override anti-discrimination protections in employment and housing.

Sherman cosponsored the Equality Act (reintroduced April 2025) providing federal protections based on gender identity and holds a 100% HRC rating. He voted against the FY2025 NDAA because it banned gender-affirming care for children of servicemembers. Sherman is a member of the Congressional Ukraine Caucus and his Russia and Belarus Financial Sanctions Act (H.R. 7066) passed the House. He appeared on CNN in February 2024 advocating continued U.S. aid and supported the April 2024 supplemental aid package including Ukraine military assistance.

Medicare Dental, Vision, and Hearing Benefit Act (March 2023) and the Keeping Our Field Offices Open Act (2025) to prevent Social Security office closures. Sherman cosponsored the Green New Deal (2019 and reintroduced 2021) to phase out fossil fuels and the West Coast Ocean Protection Act to ban offshore drilling off California. Sherman co-sponsored and voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) in 2019 and 2021 establishing automatic voter registration, expanding early voting to 15+ days, expanding mail-in voting, and making Election Day a federal holiday. He also voted for the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act (2022).

Sherman condemned Trump's DACA termination in 2017 calling it 'essentially deporting nearly 800,000 young men and women who are American in every way.' Sherman cosponsored the Green New Deal and H.Con.Res.37 declaring climate change a national emergency.
